beats.activity: Wake up, shower, coffee, and light breakfast at home.
beats.reasoning: Asa starts the week with full energy on a sunny Monday. Morning routine is brisk and purposeful, setting a productive tone.

beats.activity: Drive from home to Fremont elementary school.
beats.reasoning: Commute to work across town; Light Rail closure between Westlake and U-District won't affect Greenwood-to-Fremont route, so driving is straightforward.

beats.activity: Work as elementary school counselor: meet with students, staff coordination, and morning administrative tasks.
beats.reasoning: Full morning at school; Asa skips the 11am FIFA match (USA vs Wales) since it airs during school hours and counselor duties take priority over sports bars.

beats.activity: Drive from Fremont to Capitol Hill for lunch.
beats.reasoning: Lunch break; Asa drives toward Capitol Hill, a neighborhood with good food options and proximity to bars. Light Rail closure doesn't affect this route.

beats.activity: Lunch at a casual restaurant and catch the tail end of the FIFA match (USA vs Wales) if still on.
beats.reasoning: Asa is competitive and enjoys sports; a Capitol Hill bar is perfect for catching the second half of the USA match over lunch, though the game may be winding down by 1pm.

beats.activity: Drive from Capitol Hill back to Fremont for afternoon work.
beats.reasoning: Return to school for afternoon counseling sessions and end-of-day duties.

beats.activity: Work: afternoon counseling sessions, parent meetings, and end-of-day wrap-up.
beats.reasoning: Full afternoon of counselor work; Asa finishes the school day with strong focus.

beats.activity: Drive from school to Green Lake for evening trail run.
beats.reasoning: Sunny 74°F weather is perfect for outdoor exercise; Asa takes advantage of the beautiful evening to run the scenic Green Lake trail.

beats.activity: Trail run around Green Lake loop, enjoying the sunset and fresh air.
beats.reasoning: Competitive personality and passion for trail running drive Asa to maximize this rare clear June evening; endorphins fuel the rest of the day.
Post-run commute; Asa heads home to cool down and prepare dinner.

beats.activity: Prepare and eat dinner at home, relax on the porch with a drink.
beats.reasoning: Evening wind-down; Asa cooks a simple dinner and enjoys the lingering daylight on his porch—a generous self-care ritual after an active day.

beats.activity: Leisure at home: read, check fly-fishing reports for upcoming weekend, and plan trips.
beats.reasoning: Asa spends evening planning and indulging his passion for fly fishing; obsessive attention to detail (ferry schedules, weather, timing) shapes evening planning.
Full night of sleep (8 hours) ending just after 6am; Asa is well-rested for Tuesday after a balanced, active Monday.

diary: Monday started like most do—coffee in the kitchen, quick shower, then the drive to Fremont. The morning was steady with back-to-back student sessions; one kid was struggling with friendship drama, which never gets old. Around mid-morning I caught myself thinking about the World Cup match, so I snuck out to Monkey Loft in Capitol Hill for lunch. Caught the tail end of USA vs Wales at the bar with a sandwich. The energy downtown was different, busier than usual. Back to school for afternoon meetings with parents and more counseling work. By 2:30 I needed air, so I drove straight to Green Lake. That run was exactly what I needed—74 degrees, soft light through the trees, the loop feeling almost empty. Finished the day on my porch with a drink, scrolling fishing reports for the weekend. Felt settled.
thought_process: I knew the transit closure wouldn't affect me much since I drive, but the World Cup buzz was everywhere and I let it pull me. Taking the detour to Capitol Hill for lunch felt indulgent but right—I needed a break from the school's rhythm, something a little less predictable. The morning sessions were heavier than usual, kids processing end-of-year stress, and that drained me more than I expected. By afternoon I was running on fumes, so Green Lake wasn't optional, it was necessary. The weather was perfect, almost too perfect, which made me feel lucky. I'm looking forward to fishing this weekend, and that anticipation carried me through dinner. Overall a good day, balanced.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
